metadata.description: Comprehensive certification program for Informativ Customer Success team covering product knowledge, customer health scoring, platform navigation, and role-specific retention and expansion strategies. Divided into Foundation (all CS), CSM (credit-focused), and CSA (tier-focused) tracks.
metadata.target_audience: All Customer Success representatives (CSM and CSA)

modules.title: Informativ Product Architecture & Value Proposition
modules.description: Understanding the core Informativ product suite, how products interconnect, and the unique value proposition each delivers to automotive dealers. Covers CBC, DSGSS, SmartPull, Guardrails, and SmartPencil.
key_concepts: CBC (Credit Bureau Connection) as core credit product
DSGSS compliance platform features and use cases
SmartPull bureau optimization and intelligent bureau selection
Guardrails usage monitoring and overage management
SmartPencil digital retail integration
Product bundling and tier architecture
modules.prep_focus: Product knowledge: What each product does, what problem it solves, who benefits most. Understand how products bundle into tiers.

questions.question: Which Informativ product enables dealers to monitor credit pull usage and prevent unnecessary hard pulls?
A dealer is concerned about regulatory compliance in their F&I process. Which Informativ product directly addresses this requirement?
The Protect tier includes CBC + which compliance product?
A customer's deal is progressing through F&I, and they need immediate bureau intelligence to qualify the applicant. Which product best supports this workflow?

options.B: SmartPull smart routing and rate optimization

questions.question: What does SmartPencil add to Informativ's tier packages?

options.B: Digital retail matching in F&I workflows

modules.title: Health Score Model & Customer Signals
modules.description: Deep dive into the CI Dashboard's 5-dimension health score model (0-100 scale), how to interpret scores, revenue tier classification, and the two-track margin threshold system for tiered vs. credit customers.
key_concepts: 5 health score dimensions: Margin Health (30%), Bureau Efficiency (15%), Product Utilization (20%), Growth Trajectory (20%), Churn Risk Inverse (15%)
Health score ranges: 0-40 (high risk), 41-70 (moderate), 71-100 (healthy)
Revenue tier classification: Elite ($500K+ ARR/$41,667+ MRR), Tier 1 ($100K-$499K/$8,333-$41,666 MRR), Tier 2 ($20K-$99K/$1,667-$8,332 MRR), Tier 3 (<$20K/<$1,667 MRR)
Two-track margin thresholds: Tiered packages (Negative <0%, Critical 0-20%, Watch 20-40%, Healthy 40%+) vs. Credit (Negative <0%, Critical 0-15%, Watch 16-26%, Healthy 27%+)
Margin Health dimension: COGS ratio, seasonal variation, overage impact
Churn Risk Inverse: combination of margin, growth, and engagement signals
modules.prep_focus: Be able to read a health score and immediately assess customer risk level. Understand why credit and tiered customers have different margin thresholds. Know which dimension drives which outcomes.

questions.question: A Protect tier customer has a health score of 38. Which risk category does this fall into?
A credit customer shows a gross margin of 22%. According to the two-track threshold system, what status should this customer receive?
Which revenue tier receives a 30-day contact cadence?
Why did this healthy customer's health score drop 82→76?

options.D: Engagement patterns now trigger churn risk

questions.question: How much MRR does an Elite tier customer represent?

modules.title: CI Dashboard Navigation & Workflow
modules.description: Practical mastery of the Customer Intelligence Dashboard's 10 tabs, filter patterns, detail panel interactions, and disposition tracking workflow. Learn how to navigate from portfolio overview to individual customer deep dives.
key_concepts: Portfolio Overview: KPI cards, tier distribution, group health
Health Table: sortable customer list with health score, margin, tier, engagement status
Customer Detail Panel: T12 financials, bureau history, contact history, reprice integration
Revenue Tier Analysis: by-tier performance metrics, tier distribution trends
Cross-Sell Intelligence: expansion opportunities, product gap analysis
Bureau Optimization: pull efficiency, cost reduction strategies
Guardrails Impact: savings projections, customer impact
Disposition Tracker: contact outcomes, activity logging
CPQ Trend Alerts: quarterly allotment tracking, over/under-utilization flagging
GM Trending: margin history, seasonal patterns, recovery trajectories
modules.prep_focus: Be able to navigate the dashboard fluently. Know what each tab is for and which tab to use for a given question (e.g., 'which customers are closest to overage?' → CPQ Trend Alerts). Understand filter patterns and multi-select behavior.

questions.question: Find Tier 2 customers with negative margin. Fastest method?

options.A: Portfolio Overview → drill down by region
options.B: Revenue Tier Analysis tab filter for Tier 2
options.C: Sort Health Table by margin descending

questions.question: Panel shows monthly history but missing allotment. Limitation?

options.A: Data error; both fields are always required
options.D: Contact history and T12 margin absent

questions.question: You want to see which customers in your portfolio have the most opportunity for margin recovery through bureau optimization. Which tab do you navigate to?

The disposition tracker shows that a customer has 'Meeting Scheduled' logged 15 days ago, but you don't see evidence of a follow-up. How do you quickly identify other customers with similar overdue activity patterns?

options.A: Health Table → 'Last Engagement' column → sort ascending
options.B: My Customers Tab → Priority Queue → look for 'Overdue Touchpoints' zone
options.C: Disposition Tracker → filter by 'Meeting Scheduled' → sort by date
options.D: CS Management → Rep Accountability table → 'Overdue' column

questions.question: CPQ risk jumped 22→58 in one week. Cause?

options.D: Monthly limit reached early in quarter

modules.title: Disposition Taxonomy & Contact Cadence
modules.description: Master the complete disposition taxonomy used across Informativ platforms (CI Dashboard, PIE, Chuck), understand the distinction between contact outcomes and pipeline progress, and execute SLA-compliant cadences for different customer tiers and affiliate programs.
key_concepts: Disposition taxonomy: Contact Outcomes (Contacted-Interested, Contacted-Not Interested, No Answer, Left Voicemail, Wrong Number) / Pipeline Progress (Meeting Scheduled, Demo Completed, Proposal Sent, Negotiating, Opportunity Created) / Closed (Closed Won, Closed Lost, Not Qualified) / Data Quality (Bad Data, Duplicate, Do Not Contact)
Contact cadence by revenue tier: Elite 30 days, Tier 1 30 days, Tier 2 60 days, Tier 3 90 days
CUDL SLA for affiliate accounts: 4hr acknowledgment, 12hr first call, 24hr second attempt, 48hr outcome required
Overdue touchpoint calculation: today - last_disposition_date > tier_cadence
SLA breach definition: no solution fit meeting AND (no activity in 24hr OR >48hr with no outcome)
Disposition firing sequence: what outcome leads to next logical action
modules.prep_focus: Know the full taxonomy cold. Be able to classify any customer interaction into the correct category. Understand SLA timelines. Know how to detect overdue accounts and prioritize them.

questions.question: You call a customer and reach their voicemail. You leave a detailed message about an expansion opportunity. Which disposition should you log?

options.C: No Answer (you didn't reach the decision-maker)
options.D: Pipeline Progress / Meeting Scheduled (you positioned an opportunity)

questions.question: A Tier 2 customer was last contacted 75 days ago with a 'Meeting Scheduled' disposition. Today is day 76. What is their touchpoint status?
A CUDL affiliate prospect was assigned to you at 2:00 PM on Monday. Per CUDL SLA, your first contact attempt must be completed by what time?

options.A: 2:00 PM same day (4 hours from assignment)
options.C: 2:00 AM Wednesday (30 hours for 'next business day')

questions.question: You have three customers: (1) Elite, last contact 29 days ago; (2) Tier 1, last contact 31 days ago; (3) Tier 2, last contact 61 days ago. Which is most overdue relative to their tier cadence?
A disposition of 'Closed Lost' indicates what?

options.A: Deal was lost; customer may remain active

modules.title: Credit Bureau Economics & COGS Structure
modules.description: CSM-specific module on how credit bureau costs are calculated, how COGS impacts margin, and how the two-track margin threshold system specifically applies to credit customers. Learn to diagnose margin erosion and identify repricing opportunities.
key_concepts: Bureau pull costs: Equifax soft/hard pull rates, Experian soft/hard pull rates, TransUnion soft/hard pull rates
COGS calculation: sum of all bureau pulls + compliance reporting + processing overhead per subscription period
Gross margin calculation: (MRR Revenue - COGS) / MRR Revenue × 100% = GM%
Credit customer margin thresholds: Negative <0%, Critical 0-15%, Watch 16-26%, Healthy 27%+
Why credit margins are lower than tier packages: credit is transactional (per-pull cost), tiers are fixed (unlimited allotments)
Margin erosion drivers: increased pull volume, mix shift to expensive bureaus (hard pulls vs soft), customer onboarding creating temporary volume spike
modules.prep_focus: Understand that credit customers have inherently lower margins because they're charged per-pull. Know how volume and bureau mix directly impact COGS. Be able to explain margin thresholds to credit customers.

questions.question: A credit customer's monthly revenue is $8,000. Their monthly COGS is $5,600 (280 hard pulls × $20/hard pull). What is their gross margin percentage?
A credit customer moves from 200 pulls/month to 280 pulls/month (40% volume increase). Assuming all new pulls are hard pulls at $20 each, what is the impact to their monthly COGS?

options.D: Cannot calculate without knowing the revenue impact

questions.question: Why are credit thresholds (27%) lower than tiers (40%)?

options.A: Credit is per-pull cost; tiers are fixed
options.B: Credit deals are smaller on average

questions.question: A customer's margin just dropped from 29% to 22% (Watch threshold). You review their bureau mix and notice they've shifted from 60% soft pulls to 80% soft pulls. This change would DECREASE COGS, not increase it. What other factor likely drove the margin decline?

options.A: Bureau mix improvement should not cause margin decline
options.B: Revenue decreased (pull volume up but dealer sales down)
options.D: The calculation is wrong; soft pulls cost more than hard pulls

questions.question: Credit: 12% margin, $5K MRR. COGS drops $1,200. New margin?

modules.title: Credit Customer Retention & Margin Recovery
modules.description: Strategic playbook for identifying root causes of credit margin erosion, executing margin recovery conversations with dealers, and preventing churn through value retention. Covers SmartPull optimization and repricing methodology.
key_concepts: Root causes of margin erosion: increased pull volume (demand growth OR wasteful usage), bureau mix shift (more expensive bureaus), customer not using SmartPull optimization, unnecessary hard pulls
SmartPull optimization: soft pull rules, decision trees, reduced hard pull frequency → lower COGS
Repricing methodology: adjust cost per pull OR adjust subscription fee, communicate value delivered (bureau access, compliance), tie repricing to utilization growth
Retention conversation framework: acknowledge growth → quantify value delivered → explain cost realities → present repricing as partnership → offer optimization alternatives
Before repricing: explore optimization options (SmartPull, bureau mix review, volume analysis) as lower-friction alternative
modules.prep_focus: Master the repricing conversation. Know how to explain margin economics to a dealer. Be able to offer SmartPull as an alternative to price increases. Understand the difference between volume growth (good) and wasteful usage (bad).

questions.question: A customer's pull volume increased 30% year-over-year, but their revenue only increased 8%. What is the most likely explanation for this usage mismatch?

options.A: Dealer is running more credit pulls per deal (wasteful, not optimized)
options.B: Dealer's sales volume is actually up 30%, but billing is delayed
options.C: Informativ's pricing is inefficient for high-volume dealers
options.D: This is normal variance; volume and revenue don't correlate

questions.question: Before repricing, what to verify first?
Soft pulls only, margin still Critical. Check what?

options.A: If they need tier upgrade available

questions.question: Dealer: 'Sales grew 25%, pulls should too.' Real issue?

options.C: They are correct; growth justifies pulls

questions.question: Margin 12%→22% via SmartPull. Next action?

options.A: Upgrade to higher tier immediately now
options.C: Terminate due to risk persistent issues

modules.title: Cross-Sell & Expansion for Credit Accounts
modules.description: CSM playbook for moving credit-only customers into higher-value tier packages (Protect, Enrich, Elevate, Control) and expanding their product footprint with DSGSS compliance and SmartPencil digital retail.
key_concepts: Tier upgrade value proposition: Protect ($21.5K avg) includes DSGSS + bureau, Enrich ($28K avg) adds compliance automation, Elevate ($35K avg) adds SmartPencil, Control ($43.6K avg) adds dedicated support
Cross-sell scoring model: account for customer's current product mix, revenue tier, bureau utilization, regulatory environment
Compliance value story (DSGSS): reduce regulatory risk, streamline F&I workflows, dealer staff training, audit trails
Digital retail value story (SmartPencil): in-dealership decision support, applicant-matching, reduces decision time
Tier conversion playbook: assess fit (is customer ready for fixed-cost model?), quantify value (how much compliance risk can they avoid?), position as upgrade not upsell, offer pilot period
modules.prep_focus: Know the tier package progression and what's included at each level. Understand the value drivers for credit customers moving to tiers (compliance, efficiency, decision velocity). Be able to score a customer's tier readiness.

questions.question: Credit customer spends $6K/mo on bureau. Best tier?
Officer wants audit trails and compliance. Which product?
Credit Watch 20%. Can upgrade to Protect?

options.B: Only if they have budget available
options.C: No; Protect not for credit customers

questions.question: The SmartPencil digital retail product is most valuable to dealers in which F&I scenario?

options.A: High-volume dealerships with consistent deal pipeline
options.B: Dealerships struggling with applicant decision speed

questions.question: Control tier ($43.6K) with DSGSS. Key benefit?

modules.title: Competitive Intelligence — Credit Market
modules.description: CSM landscape awareness: competitive positioning of 700Credit, NCC, RouteOne, and DealerTrack. Know Informativ's differentiation, how to handle competitive displacement conversations, and when to escalate vs. retain.
key_concepts: 700Credit: market position (largest credit bureau player), pricing, product breadth, dealer relationships, weakness areas (compliance, digital retail)
NCC (National Credit Center): regional player, OEM focus, lower cost positioning, limited product bundling
RouteOne: integration-heavy, DMS connectivity, credit as part of larger ecosystem, lacks Informativ's compliance/digital retail
DealerTrack: payment ecosystem focus, credit secondary, not a primary competitor but relationship risk
Informativ differentiation: compliance bundle (DSGSS), digital retail (SmartPencil), customer success (CSM/CSA model), margin transparency
Displacement risk signals: long sales cycles, dealer requesting rate reductions, request for feature parity (digital retail, compliance)
modules.prep_focus: Know the competitive landscape. Understand where Informativ wins and where it loses. Know how to counter competitive threats in retention conversations. Know when to escalate to Michael vs. handle yourself.

questions.question: Competitor proposal received. Our key differentiator?

options.A: Lowest bureau costs in the market
options.D: Bundle compliance and retail into tiers

questions.question: Dealer considering NCC. Legitimate concern?

options.B: NCC pricing lower than our pricing
options.D: NCC is regional without broad coverage

questions.question: RouteOne offers DMS integration. Response?

options.A: Our compliance and retail are distinct

questions.question: Customer: 'Match 700Credit pricing?' Best answer?

options.D: We offer volume discounts they lack

questions.question: Dealer switching RouteOne for integration. Response?

options.A: Does RouteOne offer features we lack?

modules.title: Tier Architecture & CPQ Guardrails
modules.description: CSA-specific deep dive into the 5 flat-rate tier packages (Protect, Enrich, Elevate, Control, Total Solutions, Foundation), what's included in each, how CPQ allotments work, and how Guardrails usage monitoring prevents overages and margin erosion.
key_concepts: 5 tier packages: Protect (CBC+DSGSS, $21.5K avg), Enrich (Protect+automation, $28K avg), Elevate (Enrich+SmartPencil, $35K avg), Control (Elevate+dedicated support, $43.6K avg), Total Solutions (all-in), Foundation (basic)
CPQ (Credit Pull Quota) quarterly allotments: soft pull and hard pull limits per quarter, resets per quarter (Q1: Jan-Mar, Q2: Apr-Jun, etc.)
Guardrails usage monitoring: real-time pull tracking, overage alerts when approaching limits, overage charges if customer exceeds quarterly allotment
Overage pricing: per-pull rates if customer exceeds hard/soft pull allotments mid-quarter
CPQ Trend Alerts dashboard: tracks customer utilization % vs. allotment, flags customers at risk of overage before it happens
Monthly vs. quarterly cadence: allotments are quarterly, but usage is tracked monthly for early warning
modules.prep_focus: Know each tier's inclusions and pricing. Understand how CPQ works (quarterly reset, monthly tracking). Know how to prevent overages (monitor Guardrails, proactive customer communication). Be able to explain overage charges to customers.

questions.question: A Protect tier customer's quarterly CPQ allotment is 450 hard pulls and 900 soft pulls. They've used 180 hard pulls and 380 soft pulls in May (month 1 of Q2). What is their utilization rate?

options.B: 42% for hard pulls and 42% for soft pulls
options.C: 33% (180 pulls used out of 1,350 total quarterly)
options.D: Not enough data; need to know when the quarter resets

questions.question: Guardrails: 85% allotment, 2 mo left. Meaning?

options.A: Risk exceeding allotment by quarter end
options.B: Will hit overage by July 1 deadline
options.C: Usage normal for this time period

questions.question: Total Solutions: unlimited allotment. What means unlimited?

options.B: Customers pay per pull with no caps

questions.question: A Tier 2 customer is projected to exceed their Q2 hard pull allotment by 20% (overage of 120 pulls). Their overages will cost $2,400 (120 pulls × $20/pull). What should you do FIRST?

options.A: Wait until the overage occurs, then bill them
options.B: Immediately call to discuss tier upgrade or contract adjustment before overage occurs
options.C: Suggest SmartPull optimization to reduce hard pull necessity
options.D: Both B and C — offer optimization first, then discuss upgrade if needed

questions.question: CPQ Alert risk jumped sharply. Primary cause?

modules.title: Tier Customer Onboarding & Installation Success
modules.description: CSA playbook for tier customer onboarding, first 90 days success metrics, bureau settings configuration, dealer staff training, and certification milestone tracking to ensure customers realize value and reduce early churn.
key_concepts: First 90 days playbook: kick-off call (set expectations, review allotments), technical setup (bureau routing, decision logic), user training (compliance, bureau intelligence), week 2-4 utilization review, week 6-8 value realization checkpoint, 90-day business review
Bureau settings configuration: SmartPull decision trees (soft pull thresholds, bureau selection rules), bureau routing (preferred bureaus, fallback logic)
Dealer staff training: F&I staff (bureau intelligence, compliance workflows), management (usage reporting, variance analysis)
Certification milestone tracking: product certifications required (3-7 depends on tier), completion % by customer
Common failure points: dealer staff untrained on new workflow, SmartPull rules misconfigured, customer expects unlimited bureau access but has quota constraints, inadequate change management
modules.prep_focus: Master the 90-day onboarding playbook. Know how to identify configuration issues early. Understand the relationship between training completion and product success. Know how to conduct a 90-day business review that drives retention.

questions.question: Protect onboarded April 1. Success by June 1?

options.A: SmartPull configured for soft pull cost

questions.question: After 90 days, cert completion 15%. Assessment?
SmartPull threshold $20K. Correct configuration?
90-day review: 40% allotment used. Next action?
Which indicates onboarding failure by week 8?

options.A: F&I staff still use manual credit

modules.title: Tier Retention & Utilization Optimization
modules.description: CSA retention playbook for tier customers: managing utilization (preventing both under-utilization churn and over-utilization margin erosion), QBR execution, tier upgrade/downgrade decisions, and health score improvement strategies.
key_concepts: Under-utilization risk: customer uses <50% of allotment → signal of low engagement or perceived lack of value → churn risk
Over-utilization risk: customer uses >80% of allotment → approaching overage charges → margin pressure → dissatisfaction
Healthy utilization: 50-80% of allotment → customer deriving steady value, room for growth without overage
QBR (Quarterly Business Review): review allotment utilization, discuss growth/contraction, identify upsell/cross-sell, address health score trends, plan next quarter
Tier upgrade conversation: customer growing beyond allotment OR seeking more features (SmartPencil, Elevate/Control support)
Tier downgrade conversation: customer not using allotment, shrinking revenue → repositioning for success at lower tier, not as failure
Health score improvement: margin optimization (SmartPull rules), bureau efficiency (pull reduction), engagement (QBRs, training refreshers)
modules.prep_focus: Understand the sweet spot for utilization (50-80%). Know how to run a QBR that drives retention and identifies expansion. Know how to handle downgrade conversations without losing the customer.

questions.question: Protect customer: 28% allotment mid-quarter. Concern?

options.A: Usage level is healthy right now
options.C: Will hit overage by quarter end

questions.question: During a QBR, a customer reports: 'Our sales are down 15% YoY, but we're still keeping all Protect features. Can we downgrade to Foundation to save money?' How do you respond?

options.A: Fight to keep them on Protect tier; downgrades hurt revenue
options.B: Explore the root cause (market weakness vs. process issue) → if market-driven, suggest downgrade to Foundation as bridge during downturn
options.C: Recommend Foundation, but position as a 90-day test with easy upgrade path when business rebounds
options.D: Both B and C — explore root cause, offer downgrade as bridge, set clear upgrade triggers

questions.question: Health dropped 78→67, margin unchanged. What?
A Tier 2 customer is at 76% of their quarterly hard pull allotment with 6 weeks left in the quarter. They've maintained this pace for 2 quarters. What is the optimal recommendation?

options.A: Monitor closely for overage, prepare for billing
options.B: Suggest upgrade to Tier 1 or Control for more headroom
options.C: This is healthy utilization; they're deriving value
options.D: Implement SmartPull optimization to reduce hard pull dependency

questions.question: During a QBR, the customer asks: 'Why can't we get SmartPencil features at Protect tier pricing?' What is the most defensible response?

options.A: SmartPencil is an Elevate+ feature; bundling drives value-based pricing
options.B: Informativ could offer SmartPencil add-on for $8,000/month if they want the feature
options.C: Suggest upgrade to Elevate ($35K) which includes SmartPencil alongside improved bureau efficiency
options.D: Any of the above, depending on the customer's specific pain point

modules.title: Advanced Tier Value Realization & Enterprise Account Management
modules.description: Capstone CSA module on advanced tier strategy: SmartPencil digital retail workflows, DSGSS compliance deep dive, multi-rooftop group pricing, ROI measurement and communication, and elite tier ($500K+) enterprise account management.
key_concepts: SmartPencil digital retail: in-dealership applicant-matching, decision velocity improvement, F&I staff productivity gains, measurable ROI (deals/day increase, F&I close rates)
DSGSS compliance: audit trails, regulatory reporting, staff training integration, risk reduction quantification
Multi-rooftop group pricing: dealer group consolidation, volume-based pricing tiers, group-level health scorecards
ROI measurement: deals closed per month, average deal size, F&I speed, margin improvement, churn prevention value
ROI communication: quantify in $ saved (compliance risk, decision time), $ earned (faster F&I closes, upsell attach), ROI multiple over contract term
Elite tier ($500K+ ARR) management: dedicated CSM assigned, quarterly business reviews, strategic account planning, C-level relationship building
modules.prep_focus: Master the advanced value story. Know how to measure and articulate ROI to dealers. Understand group pricing dynamics. Know how to manage and grow Elite tier relationships (these are $6M+ contracts if kept for 10 years).

questions.question: SmartPencil: 13 min saved per deal. Monthly FTE?
A dealer group owns 6 franchises. Informativ provides Protect tier service to each. What is the primary business case for consolidating to a group-level contract?

options.A: Lower per-rooftop pricing due to volume commitment
options.B: Simplified billing and central account management
options.C: Both A and B — volume pricing + operational consolidation
options.D: Higher allotment across the group (6× single-rooftop allotments)

questions.question: Elite ($500K+), 18 months tenure, health 89→62. Priority?
A DSGSS compliance customer asks: 'How do we measure the value of compliance infrastructure?' What is the most concrete answer?

options.A: Regulatory risk reduction (hard to quantify but critical)
options.B: Audit preparation time reduced by 60% = $15K/year labor savings + risk mitigation
options.C: Compliance is intangible value; focus on the credit and digital retail ROI instead
options.D: Compliance value is specific to the dealer's regulatory environment

questions.question: Elite customer considering downgrade. Response?
